message.reasoning_content:
We need to evaluate each candidate and decide if it is a valid ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge item. The instruction: "Accept an item only if it is useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. Reject legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence."

We are to output JSON with verdicts. For each, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge boolean, and reason (short). If not valid, provide category_if_not from allowed list: null, front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other.

Let's go through each candidate.

cand_a4202852974f5541: "Supply Fan Start/Stop Logic", section 5.17.1.1, summary: "The supply fan runs during Warmup and Setback modes, and during Occupied mode while there are any heating-fan requests, with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes." Evidence quote: "Fan shall run when system is in the Warmup Mode and Setback Mode , and during Occupied Mode while there are any heating -fan requests with a minimum runtime of 15 minutes." This is a direct operational sequence from the guideline. It seems valid and grounded. Accept.

cand_870a7f843eeb9eda: "Totalize VAV Airflow for Diagnostics", 5.17.1.1, summary: "Sum VAV box airflow rates to obtain overall supply air rate without AFMS, used for display, diagnostics, and filter alarm." Evidence: "Totalize current airflow rate from VAV boxes to a software point. VAV box airflow rates are summed to obtain overall supply air rate without the need for an AFMS at the air -handler discharge. This is used only for display and diagnostics and filter alarm." This is also operational, used for diagnostics. Accept.

cand_2c54206d5f6c70f2: "Static Pressure Setpoint Reset", 5.17.1.2, summary: "The static pressure setpoint is reset using Trim & Respond logic with the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2." Evidence: "Static pressure setpoint . Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14 ) using the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2." This is a valid operational sequence, referring to a table. Accept.

cand_b0bd7283368a1895: "Trim & Respond Parameters for Static Pressure Reset", 5.17.1.2, summary lists specific T&R parameters. Evidence: Table 5.17.1.2. This is a parameter spec, directly from the guideline. Accept.

cand_fa208c8c7b06a809: "Supply Air Temperature Control Loop Enable", 5.17.2.1, summary: "The control loop is enabled when supply air fan is proven ON, and disabled and output set to zero otherwise." Evidence: "Control loop is enabled when the supply air fan is proven ON, and disabled and output set to zero otherwise." Valid operational sequence. Accept.

cand_e1e5ca3332ed5b74: "Trim & Respond Parameters for Heating SAT Reset", 5.17.2.2, summary and evidence from Table 5.17.2.2. Valid parameter spec. Accept.

cand_97de9b3e0df39dc6: "Heating SAT Setpoint in Warmup/Setback Modes", 5.17.2.2, summary: "During Warmup Mode and Setback Mode, the heating SAT setpoint is Max_HtgSAT." Evidence: "During Warmup Mode and Setback Mode . Setpoint shall be Max_HtgSAT." Valid operational sequence. Accept.

cand_a3f9968d76c6b33e: "Heating Coil PID Control", 5.17.2.3, summary: "Supply air temperature is maintained at setpoint by a PID loop modulating the heating coil." Evidence: "Supply air temperature shall be maintained at setpoint by a PID loop modulating the heating coil." Valid operational sequence. Accept.

cand_fc0716d5d21d92d5: "Fan Maintenance Interval Alarm", 5.17.3.1, summary: "Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged." Evidence: "Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged." This is a fault diagnostic rule. Valid. Accept.

cand_ed2ccbca3113fb4c: "Fan Status Alarm", 5.17.3.2, summary: "Fan alarm is indicated by status different from command for 15 seconds. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2; Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4." Evidence: same. Valid fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

cand_cfa0aae7457a13a3: "Filter Pressure Drop Alarm", 5.17.3.3, summary: "Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of alarm limit or 12.5 Pa for 10 minutes when airflow exceeds 20%: Level 4. Alarm limit varies with total airflow as DPx = DP100 * x^1.4." Evidence: similar. Valid fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

cand_757c9d3e26a6cc8c: "Required Points for DFDD Heating AHU AFDD", 5.17.4.2, summary: "AFDD requires SAT, RAT, DSP, SATSP, DSPSP, HC, FS points available." Evidence: "5.17.4.2. The following points must be availabl e to the AFDD routines for each AHU:" This is a parameter spec, listing required points. Accept.

cand_f0611c8e3aa2e5c0: "Rolling Averages for AFDD", 5.17.4.3, summary: "Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling time are calculated for SAT, RAT, DSP. Operator adjustable averaging window and sampling period." Evidence: "Five-minute rolling averages with 1 -minute sampling time of the following point values; operator shall have the ability to adjust the averaging window and sampling period for each point independently...". This is an operational sequence (or parameter spec) for calculations. Accept.

cand_d2de159139592a7b: "DFDD Heating AHU AFDD Internal Variables", 5.17.4.4, summary lists internal variables with default values. Evidence: "5.17.4.4. The internal variables shown in Table 5.17.4.4 shall be defined for each AHU. All parameters are". It provides specific defaults. Valid parameter spec. Accept.

cand_4167a4c4d4724d2c: "Fault Condition 1: Duct Static Pressure Too Low at Full Fan Speed", 5.17.4.5, summary and evidence from Table 5.17.4.5. Valid fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

cand_ab1047fb33d0359c: "Fault Condition 2: SAT Too Low in Full Heating", 5.17.4.5, valid fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

cand_ee9d7af4747ee778: "Fault Condition 3: Temperature Rise Across Inactive Heating Coil", 5.17.4.5. Evidence: "5.17.4.5. Table 5.17.4.5 shows potential fault conditions that can be evaluated by the AFDD routines." The summary describes the condition: "If RAT_avg - SAT_avg ≥ sqrt(εSAT²+εRAT²) + ΔTSF and HC = 0%, then fault: temperature rise across inactive heating coil." The evidence quote is not specific to this fault condition; it just says the table shows conditions. But the summary likely comes from the table. However, we need to see if it's supported by evidence. The candidate gives a summary, and the evidence quote is generic. The instruction says "reject summaries not supported by evidence." The evidence quote does not include the specific condition, just a reference to the table. That might be considered unsupported because the evidence does not actually quote the condition. But the table content is part of the guideline, and the evidence quote might be a snippet. The title and section are given. We might consider the candidate as having insufficient evidence? The evidence quote should directly support the summary. Here, the evidence quote does not contain the fault condition equation. The summary seems to be derived from the table, but the evidence is not provided. This could be considered "unsupported" because the evidence doesn't back the specific claim. However, the candidate is based on Guideline 36, and we are to validate extracted items. Possibly the evidence is truncated. The instruction says "reject ... summaries not supported by evidence." So if the evidence quote is not sufficient, reject. I'll check: evidence quote: "5.17.4.5. Table 5.17.4.5 shows potential fault conditions that can be evaluated by the AFDD routines." That is not enough to support the specific fault condition summary. So I'll mark as unsupported. But note that the candidate_id might be from a valid extraction, but the evidence provided in the JSON is what we have to judge. So yes, reject as unsupported.

cand_83e36378b3c890e6: "Fault Condition Alarm Suppression", 5.17.4.6, summary: "For each air handler, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition." Evidence: "For each air handler, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition." That is a direct quote. Valid operational sequence. Accept.

cand_4f2bbbc86f80cd6e: "Fault Condition Evaluation Suspension", 5.17.4.7, summary: "Evaluation of fault conditions is suspended when AHU is not operating, and for ModeDelay minutes after a change in mode of any Zone Group served." Evidence: "Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions: a. When AHU is not operating b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode ..." Direct. Accept.

cand_6eac00e2f432a7e3: "Fault Condition Alarm Delay", 5.17.4.8, summary: "A fault condition that evaluates as TRUE must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator." Evidence: same. Accept.

cand_e824a80a8c07fed5: "AFDD Test Mode", 5.17.4.9, summary: "Test mode temporarily sets ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of AFDD, then normal fault detection resumes." Evidence: similar. Accept.

cand_835e0f9c8f53467c: "Fault Condition Alarm Reporting", 5.17.4.10, summary: "When a fault condition is reported, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and include the fault description and list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.17.4.5." Evidence: direct quote. Accept.

cand_c3d7153a402b9321: "Hot Water Valve Overrides for Commissioning", 5.17.5, summary: "Provide software switches that interlock to CHW and hot-water plant level to force hot water valve full open and full closed." Evidence: similar. Valid commissioning step. Accept.

Now, check for duplicates: None appear to be duplicates.

Thus, only cand_ee9d7af4747ee778 might be rejected due to unsupported evidence. The summary is plausible but the evidence quote doesn't match. So I'll set is_valid=false, reason="evidence quote does not support the specific fault condition", category_if_not="unsupported".

All others valid.

Output JSON accordingly.
